{{Quote|Pay attention, kids; that's how it's done!|Dax Gazaway|<ref name="Racer Revenge" />}}
'''Dax Gazaway''' was a [[Podracing/Legends|Podracing]] announcer who covered many races on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] prior to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] during the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]]. Dax's hometown was [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]], a [[City/Legends|city]] located on the Outer Rim [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. He was said to know every [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|pilot]] in the circuits he broadcast.

==Biography==
Dax Gazaway was born in the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]], located on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. A bustling town, Mos Espa was known for the annual [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] [[Podracing/Legends|Podrace]], which attracted fans from across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. By [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]], Gazaway had picked up the profession of Podrace broadcaster and in-race commentator. Gazaway followed the 24 BBY Podracing season, which saw a number of seasoned professionals return to the track alongside a number of newcomers. The season was most notable for the return of the [[Dug/Legends|Dug]] [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|pilot]] [[Sebulba/Legends|Sebulba]], entering the season with a massive [[Collor Pondrat/Legends|Collor Pondrat]] [[Plug-G Gargantua|Plug-G ''Gargantua'' Split-X]] [[Podracer/Legends|Podracer]], and his old nemesis, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], a former [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] who was by now a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] in training.<ref name="Racer Revenge" />

Gazaway followed the competition across the various circuits and courses, broadcasting the races. Gazaway would usually begin the race, previewing some of the course's features before sending the [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] off. He would then partake in color commentary, remarking on the behavior of certain pilots on the track, as well riffing crashes with numerous metaphors.<ref name="Racer Revenge" /> Despite his occasionally callous remarks, he was said to have had a working relationship with every pilot in the tournament. At the season's end, Gazaway traveled to the [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|palace]] of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] to announce the season's victor.

==Behind the scenes==
Dax Gazaway first appeared as the race commentator in the [[2002]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Racer Revenge]] and was voiced by [[Tom Kane]].<ref name="Racer Revenge" /> Gazaway [[Tuckerization|shares his name with]] former [[LucasArts]] level designer Dax Gazaway. During development of ''[[Star Wars: Racer Revenge]]'', a name was needed for the Podracing announcer, and Gazaway allowed his to be used.<ref name="Blogspot">{{Blogspot|subdomain=georgesgamedesign|url=2011/11/interview-2-dax-gazaway.html|name=George's Game Design|text=INTERVIEW #2: DAX GAZAWAY}}</ref>
